title: meaning of tabbris the angel of self determination
display_title: meaning of tabbris the angel of self determination
wikitext:
[]: meaning of tabbris the angel of self determination             Quote Post by brainissues » Wed Sep 16, 2009 5:26 pm DOMINION: This angel holds dominion over free will, choice, alternatives and self-determination. Tabbris inspires the ability to clearly invision and choose creative alternatives. GUIDANCE: Angel of Self-Determination SAMPLE AFFIRMATION: I AM using my free will to choose the perfect and positive outcomes. SAMPLE PRAYER: Angel of Free Will and Self-Determination, through knee-jerk reactions and lack in judgment I have made some choices in my Life that made my path difficult. I now consciously and knowingly choose an easier, lighter and abundant path with your assistance. I choose to use my free will to do the most Good and arriving at the most perfect outcome.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Sep 16, 2009 6:55 pm NAME: Tabris DOMAIN: Free Will, Self-Determination, Choice, and Alternatives SEX: Male AGE: Unknown (Disguised appearance of about 14 or 15) APPEARANCE: Since Tabris spends a good amount of his time working with mankind, He granted Tabris a "human disguise" PREFERRED WEAPON: Heaven's Cloud, a massive sword with a golden blade, capable of bringing light or darkness to the skies POWERS: Divine Intervention - Allows the person in question to see the consequences of his actions, and suggests an alternative (works for both Good and Evil) Only In Your Dreams - Gives the targeted person their deepest desire in their dreams, and leaves a subliminal message about obtaining it (mainly used for converting Good to Evil) BACKGROUND: On the surface, Tabris appears to be the typical "Good" angel. In reality though, he is more Neutral that anything. He is in regular contact between both the Holy and Fallen Angels, he acts as a go-between to stave off massive conflicts, and also works as an information dealer for both sides. Seeing as how he is the Angel of Free Will, he has his own agenda and leaves spiritual conquest to the Warrior Angels. However, during the First Conflict, he fought right along side Michael and Gabriel. He used the mighty blade given to him to bring light to the darkened battlefield and eradicate those who dared opposed Him. During the conflict, he came to realize something. Even though the Fallen Angels were Evil and going against God, they were still Angels. He had this "enlightening" only after he saw who was leading the Fallen Angels, an Angel who he had feelings for (not that she ever knew), Lucifer. Instead of becoming a Fallen Angel, Tabris merely resigned from the battlefield and allowed himself to be sucked into the vortex to Earth. Now, he resides in an old area of Shinjuku working as a "fortune teller." He's given a daily list of people and to which side they belong and should belong to. He uses his powers to guide those humans towards the path chosen for them by Heaven or Hell. He has long since forgotten about his crush on Lucifer and has been trying to get together with someone else. His once mighty Angelic weapon has been reduced to paper-weight and flashlight duty.

[]:             Quote Post by brainissues » Wed Sep 16, 2009 8:20 pm tnkz for the extensive concept , if anybody has more information it will be better Cya

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 16, 2009 8:35 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Sep 16, 2009 9:32 pm :-{|

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Sep 16, 2009 9:46 pm This thread.....your thread.....

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Sep 16, 2009 10:21 pm My new and improved forum senses tell that a lock may be near if not iminent.

[]:             Quote Post by DEMOLITION D+ » Thu Sep 17, 2009 3:20 am Well I'm genuiniely confused

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Sep 17, 2009 6:18 am I'll take a wild guess and say the topic starter wants information on Kaworu/Tabris... I think?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 17, 2009 1:26 pm It doesn't mean anything, because the Angels of Evangelion aren't literally Angels and their titles mean absolutely jack-shit.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Sep 17, 2009 1:28 pm ^ My point exactly. That, and there's a lot of stuff you can Google.

[]:             Quote Post by brainissues » Fri Sep 18, 2009 9:16 pm after all the critics , i'm triyin' to create some biblical profile of tabriz , tabris , tabbris , so before the commentaries , WTF is this , i will think it twice , it was only to make an apart wiki before this angel , ah , another thing , they are not angels , the correct word should be apostol , vaya con dios dio con andare il faut rendre à César ce qui est à César,

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Apr 04, 2012 8:54 am The names don't mean "jack shit". The design of the angels are based off of the angels holding their names. Tabris is the angel of free will, which is why they chose that name to go with Kaworu, who firmly claims that he has a will and can choose to not fuse with Lilith, unlike the other angels which are forced to follow their survival instincts against the Lilin invaders of their planet. Another example is the Mind-Rape angel, which is named after the Angel of Birds. The angel that cries acid is the angel of rain, the bomb angel that falls from the sky is the Angel of the Sky, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 04, 2012 2:07 pm The names don't mean "jack shit". The design of the angels are based off of the angels holding their names.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Apr 04, 2012 2:16 pm I hear that Ramiel is the Angel of Thunder. Anyway; yeah. As with the rest of the religious symbolism, it's more superficial than meaningful. At best it can be thematic in some instances. At the very least, Tabris's naming is representative of his choice to not cause Third Impact (from a writing perspective). At most, it's representative of what I posted above, which I'd rather assume is what the authors were insinuating. The angel that infected Rei was able to speak, and it showed to not have very much in its head other than its biological imperative to infect and expand, like any other virus (emotionally feeding off of Rei was just a consequence of its integration with the her). It's kind of like the angel that infected the Magi- it was only acting towards its own survival. We also have to remember that the Humans hold the fruit of Wisdom, not the Angels, and so necessarily they are not intelligent, are also probably not free agents, as one needs to be intelligent enough to be not acting purely on instinct. Whether or not Tabris has Free Will because he has received a human brain, is a higher angel since he used to be Adam, or because it is simply his 'special ability' as the 17th angel, is up in the air. Tabris has an unfair advantage because he has a mouth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 05, 2012 2:32 pm The angel that infected Rei was able to speak, and it showed to not have very much in its head other than its biological imperative to infect and expand, like any other virus (emotionally feeding off of Rei was just a consequence of its integration with the her). It's kind of like the angel that infected the Magi- it was only acting towards its own survival.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Thu Apr 05, 2012 3:31 pm Kaworu could have just as well continued his human act indefinitely if it weren't for Seele, and so could have become a contributing member of human society. If your idea of the Fruit of Knowledge is true, Kaworu would still end up possessing it as much as any other individual human- so we run into problems here whichever of the two ways we look at it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Apr 06, 2012 2:39 pm Kaworu could have just as well continued his human act indefinitely if it weren't for Seele, and so could have become a contributing member of human society.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Apr 06, 2012 7:38 pm did anyone else misread this thread title as 'the angel of self destruction '? Nope? Must just have been me

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Fri Apr 06, 2012 7:40 pm EvangelionFan wrote: did anyone else misread this thread title as 'the angel of self destruction '? Nope? Must just have been me
